metadata.title: Niraparib and Abiraterone acetate (Akeega®) and prednisoLONE Therapy
metadata.indication: Niraparib/abiraterone with prednisolone for BRCA1/2-mutated mCRPC when chemotherapy is not clinically indicated.
metadata.partial_assessment_note: Any independently actionable entered value is assessed; omitted domains remain explicitly unassessed and never block a partial result.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: Treatment plan must be initiated by the appropriately qualified Consultant specified in the NCCP source.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification or non-standard continuation should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: Decision-support encoding of the current NCCP source. It does not replace the official NCCP regimen, clinical judgement, local policy, prescribing review or pharmacy verification.

haematologic_toxicity_grade.label: Worst current haematological toxicity

options.label: Grade 0 — No toxicity
options.description: No clinical or laboratory evidence of this adverse event.

options.label: Grade 1 — Mild
options.description: Mild or asymptomatic; observation or diagnostic review only; intervention generally not indicated.

options.label: Grade 2 — Moderate
options.description: Moderate; minimal, local or non-invasive intervention may be indicated; limits instrumental activities of daily living.

options.label: Grade 3 — Severe
options.description: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalisation may be indicated; limits self-care activities of daily living.

haematologic_toxicity_grade.assessment_guidance: Identify the exact adverse event, assess symptoms and functional impact, and apply the toxicity-specific CTCAE v5.0 definition. Do not grade solely from a generic severity impression.

anaemia_grade.label: Anaemia grade

options.label: Grade 0 — No anaemia
options.description: Haemoglobin within the relevant normal range and no anaemia.

options.label: Grade 1 — Grade 1
options.description: Haemoglobin below lower limit of normal to 10 g/dL.

options.label: Grade 3 — Grade 3
options.description: Haemoglobin <8 g/dL; transfusion may be indicated.

anaemia_grade.assessment_guidance: Identify the exact adverse event, assess symptoms and functional impact, and apply the toxicity-specific CTCAE v5.0 definition. Do not grade solely from a generic severity impression.

nonhaem_toxicity_grade.label: Worst current non-haematological toxicity

options.label: Grade 0 — No toxicity
options.description: No clinical or laboratory evidence of this adverse event.

options.label: Grade 1 — Mild
options.description: Mild or asymptomatic; observation or diagnostic review only; intervention generally not indicated.

options.label: Grade 2 — Moderate
options.description: Moderate; minimal, local or non-invasive intervention may be indicated; limits instrumental activities of daily living.

options.label: Grade 3 — Severe
options.description: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalisation may be indicated; limits self-care activities of daily living.

nonhaem_toxicity_grade.assessment_guidance: Identify the exact adverse event, assess symptoms and functional impact, and apply the toxicity-specific CTCAE v5.0 definition. Do not grade solely from a generic severity impression.

alt_ast_uln_multiple.label: ALT / AST result (actual value entered in UI; highest ×ULN calculated automatically)

bilirubin_uln_multiple.label: Bilirubin result (actual value entered in UI; ×ULN calculated automatically)

renal_band.label: Renal function category

options.label: Severe impairment — benefit-risk review and close monitoring
End-stage renal disease / haemodialysis — benefit-risk review and close monitoring

renal_band.assessment_guidance: Select the protocol-specific renal category matching the verified CrCl/eGFR. Exact numerical entry is not required because the NCCP decision is categorical.

hys_law_pattern.label: ALT/AST >3 ×ULN with bilirubin >2 ×ULN without another cause

output_templates.proceed: No encoded restriction was triggered in the entered domain(s). This is not an overall treatment clearance.
output_templates.coverage_gap: Only entered values were assessed; all omitted clinically relevant domains remain unassessed.
output_templates.consultant_review: The entered value requires protocol-specific clinical review.
output_templates.withhold: Withhold and reassess according to the official NCCP pathway.
output_templates.delay: Delay treatment and reassess according to the official NCCP pathway.

action.message: Withhold and monitor at least weekly until Grade 1/baseline; then resume according to occurrence and dose-level table.

rules.explanation: Withhold and monitor at least weekly until Grade 1/baseline; then resume according to occurrence and dose-level table.

action.message: Second Grade ≥3 haematological occurrence: withhold to recovery then reduce one dose level.

rules.explanation: Second Grade ≥3 haematological occurrence: withhold to recovery then reduce one dose level.

action.message: Third Grade ≥3 haematological occurrence: permanently discontinue.

rules.explanation: Third Grade ≥3 haematological occurrence: permanently discontinue.

action.message: Withhold, provide supportive management and monitor at least weekly until recovered to Grade ≤2; reduce for recurrence/persistence as specified.

rules.explanation: Withhold, provide supportive management and monitor at least weekly until recovered to Grade ≤2; reduce for recurrence/persistence as specified.

action.message: Interrupt for Grade ≥3 non-haematological toxicity until Grade 1/baseline; resume with dose review.

rules.explanation: Interrupt for Grade ≥3 non-haematological toxicity until Grade 1/baseline; resume with dose review.

action.message: ALT/AST >5 ×ULN: interrupt; after recovery to baseline, re-treat at one dose reduction. Discontinue if it recurs.

rules.explanation: ALT/AST >5 ×ULN: interrupt; after recovery to baseline, re-treat at one dose reduction. Discontinue if it recurs.

action.message: Permanently discontinue for ALT/AST >3 ×ULN with bilirubin >2 ×ULN without another cause.

rules.explanation: Permanently discontinue for ALT/AST >3 ×ULN with bilirubin >2 ×ULN without another cause.

action.message: Severe renal impairment/ESRD: use only if benefit outweighs risk with close monitoring.

rules.explanation: Severe renal impairment/ESRD: use only if benefit outweighs risk with close monitoring.

action.message: Child-Pugh B requires cautious individual benefit-risk assessment.

rules.explanation: Child-Pugh B requires cautious individual benefit-risk assessment.

action.message: Akeega is contraindicated in severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C).

rules.explanation: Akeega is contraindicated in severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C).

action.message: Correct potassium before treatment; maintain ≥4 mmol/L where possible.

rules.explanation: Correct potassium before treatment; maintain ≥4 mmol/L where possible.

action.message: Optimise potassium to ≥4 mmol/L and monitor closely.

rules.explanation: Optimise potassium to ≥4 mmol/L and monitor closely.

action.message: Prior PARP inhibitor treatment is an NCCP exclusion.

rules.explanation: Prior PARP inhibitor treatment is an NCCP exclusion.

action.message: Current or suspected MDS/AML is an NCCP exclusion.

rules.explanation: Current or suspected MDS/AML is an NCCP exclusion.

administration.dose: 200 mg/1000 mg once daily (two 100 mg/500 mg tablets)
administration.instructions: Take as a single daily dose on an empty stomach as specified in the NCCP source.

dose_reductions.haematological: Two 50 mg/500 mg tablets once daily; second reduction consider discontinuation.
dose_reductions.hepatotoxicity: One 100 mg/500 mg tablet once daily; second reduction discontinue.

treatment.additional_requirement: Continue GnRH analogue unless surgically castrated.

eligibility: mCRPC with germline and/or somatic BRCA1/2 mutation

regular: FBC weekly for first month then monthly as specified
Liver profile and blood pressure at protocol intervals

supportive_care.mapping_basis: Akeega is classified as oral moderate-high emetogenic risk.
supportive_care.premedications: Not usually required; consider prophylactic antiemetics for the first 2 weeks as clinically indicated.
